seo.title: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) tools directory
seo.description: A developer-first directory of RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) tools and references.

content.intro: A curated collection of production-grade tools and libraries for building, optimizing, and evaluating Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines. This directory focuses on high-performance vector stores, orchestration frameworks, and evaluation suites required for enterprise AI applications.

entries.name: Pinecone
entries.description: Managed, cloud-native vector database designed for high-performance similarity search with serverless scaling.
pros: Low latency retrieval for large-scale datasets
cons: Can become expensive with high throughput

entries.name: Qdrant
entries.description: Open-source vector similarity search engine and database written in Rust, offering a high-performance API.
cons: Requires manual infrastructure management for self-hosting

entries.name: pgvector
entries.description: Open-source vector similarity search extension for PostgreSQL, enabling RAG directly within existing relational databases.
pros: Minimal learning curve for SQL developers
cons: Limited specialized vector features compared to dedicated DBs
Performance may lag on ultra-large datasets

entries.name: LlamaIndex
entries.description: Data-centric framework for connecting custom data sources to large language models with advanced indexing strategies.
pros: Deep focus on complex data retrieval and indexing
Built-in support for query engines and routers
cons: Steeper learning curve for advanced index types
Frequent API changes due to rapid development

entries.name: LangChain
entries.description: Comprehensive framework for building LLM applications using composable chains and pre-built components for RAG.
pros: Integrates with nearly every major AI tool
cons: Heavy abstractions can make debugging difficult
Documentation can lag behind library updates

entries.name: Ragas
entries.description: Framework that provides metrics for evaluating RAG pipelines, focusing on faithfulness, relevance, and answer correctness.
pros: Specific metrics for retrieval and generation components
cons: Requires LLM calls for evaluation, incurring costs
Evaluation quality depends on the judge model

entries.name: Cohere Rerank
entries.description: A powerful reranking model that re-orders search results to improve the precision of the top-k retrieved documents.
pros: Easy integration with existing vector search workflows
cons: Adds additional latency to the RAG pipeline

entries.name: Unstructured.io
entries.description: Open-source libraries and APIs to ingest and preprocess unstructured data (PDFs, HTML, etc.) for RAG applications.
pros: Excellent at parsing complex document layouts
Pre-built connectors for various data sources
Removes boilerplate code for document cleaning
cons: Resource intensive for self-hosting local models

entries.name: DeepEval
entries.description: Testing framework for LLM applications that allows developers to write unit tests for RAG outputs.
cons: Requires setup for local testing environments

entries.name: Voyage AI
entries.description: High-performance embedding models optimized for retrieval accuracy and specific domains like finance and law.
cons: Less known compared to OpenAI embeddings
